computational_readiness.target: Fe-S cluster biogenesis x Circadian clock regulation

checks.name: Nernst equation: 30mV redox oscillation, n=1, T=310K
checks.detail: exp(30/26.71) = 3.07-fold Kd shift in [2Fe-2S] cluster stability. Mathematically correct. Sensitivity: 20mV gives 2.11x, 40mV gives 4.47x. Even conservative 20mV is biologically meaningful.

checks.name: Labile cluster half-life vs 24h circadian period
checks.detail: t_half 2-8h gives 31-80% amplitude tracking of 24h oscillation. ISCU2 (~1h) = 94% tracking. CISD2 (~2h) = 80%. GLRX5/FDX2 (~4h) = 55%. IRP1 (~3h) = 66%. All assembly hub proteins in high-sensitivity window.

checks.name: NFS1 Km sensitivity to cysteine oscillation
checks.detail: NFS1 Km ~50uM, [Cys] ~150uM. +/-30% cysteine oscillation gives only 17% activity change. Primary mechanism more likely direct redox modification of NFS1 Cys328, not substrate driven.

checks.name: KEGG pathway overlap: Fe-S (hsa04122) vs Circadian (hsa04710)
checks.detail: Zero shared genes. CISD2 and GLRX5 absent from all KEGG pathways. AMPK is the nearest indirect bridge (2-3 steps). Confirms genuine novelty.

checks.name: STRING protein interactions: Fe-S proteins vs clock proteins
checks.detail: Zero interactions at any confidence threshold between NFS1/CISD2/GLRX5/FDX2 and BMAL1/CRY1/PER2/CLOCK. Confirms disjointness. Mechanism is metabolic flux, not PPI.

checks.name: PubMed co-occurrence: GLRX5 AND circadian
checks.detail: 0 papers. NFS1 AND circadian = 1 (non-mechanistic). CISD2 AND circadian = 1 (coincidental). Compared to heme AND circadian = 203.

checks.name: Compartmentalization: mitochondrial Fe-S assembly vs nuclear clock
checks.detail: NFS1/ISCU2 are mitochondrial. CRY1/BMAL1 are nuclear. Bridge via: Clock -> transcription of NADPH enzymes -> mitochondrial redox -> NFS1/ISCU2 activity -> GLRX5/CIA export to cytoplasm -> IRP1 switch. Established export pathway exists.

checks.name: IRP1 aconitase/IRE-BP switch as measurable readout
checks.detail: IRP1 is [4Fe-4S] protein switching to IRE-binding form when cluster lost. t_half ~3h = 66% circadian tracking. Nadimpalli 2024 already confirmed diurnal IRE-mRNA control. Validates concept.

warnings: CRY1/2 uses FAD, NOT Fe-S clusters. Do not propose CRY-Fe-S direct binding.
2026 JCI paper (PMID 41480765) shows BMAL1 -> copper -> Fe-S protein stability in glioblastoma. Differentiate from biogenesis mechanism.
NFS1 cysteine substrate oscillation alone is MARGINAL (17% effect). Primary mechanism should be direct redox modification of NFS1 active site.
CISD2 and GLRX5 absent from KEGG - database gap, not biological absence.
recommendations: Prioritize NFS1 activity oscillation via direct Cys328 redox modification, not substrate Km
IRP1/ACO1 aconitase switch is the best measurable readout (validated diurnal by Nadimpalli 2024)
CISD2/NAF-1 longevity x circadian connection has zero prior work - high novelty
Use two NFS1 inhibitors (Compound 53, D-cysteine) as ready experimental tools
Reference 2012 Drosophila paper as key lead, 2026 JCI paper as nearest mammalian precedent
Build tiered half-life spectrum as core prediction: assembly proteins oscillate, structural proteins don't
